The title “AI’s Deflation and Prosperity – Seminar Series 1” suggests the first installment of a discussion exploring the dual, seemingly paradoxical economic effects of artificial intelligence:  

- **AI-Driven Deflation**: AI boosts productivity, automates labor-intensive tasks, and reduces marginal costs (e.g., in software, content creation, customer service), leading to downward pressure on prices for many goods and services — a deflationary force.  
- **AI-Driven Prosperity**: Simultaneously, AI spurs innovation, enables new industries and business models, enhances human capabilities, and can raise long-term growth, wages (for complementing skills), and overall economic welfare — a prosperity-enhancing force.  

The seminar likely frames this tension as central to understanding AI’s macroeconomic impact — challenging conventional trade-offs, redefining inflation metrics, and raising policy questions about distribution, labor transitions, and investment in human capital and infrastructure.

*Note:* Without the full transcript or slides, this summary reflects the conceptual framing implied by the title and typical themes in such AI-economics seminars.
